mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 6.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in greater than 55 percent of instances when the unemployment rate exceeds the 6.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based demand contraction, leading to negative corporate earnings revisions and negative forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts yield positive 60-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production above 0.5 percent signals accelerating supply-side momentum, triggering short-term positive equity repricing over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 50 percent of instances when industrial production month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a stagnant federal funds rate creates a negative real yield environment, prompting equity market sell-offs due to anticipated aggressive future tightening over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of instances when the inflation-rate divergence condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue potential, generating positive forward equity returns over a 60 trading day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no statistical significance against baseline during payroll growth periods, with a t-test p-value exceeding 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent indicates severe economic stagnation, triggering negative equity returns as investors price in recession risks over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized crosses below 1.0 percent
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day forward returns in greater than 55 percent of quarters where annualized GDP growth falls below the 1.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment signals a supply-demand imbalance that boosts corporate margins, driving short-term positive equity returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 50 percent of instances when the supply-labor divergence condition is simultaneously satisfied.
